steps.title: Use tangent's period
steps.content: Tangent has period $\pi$, so subtract multiples of $\pi$ until the angle is familiar. $$\frac{92\pi}{3}=30\pi+\frac{2\pi}{3}$$ Therefore, $$\tan\frac{92\pi}{3}=\tan\frac{2\pi}{3}$$

steps.title: Evaluate the reference angle
steps.content: $\frac{2\pi}{3}$ is in quadrant II with reference angle $\frac{\pi}{3}$. Tangent is negative in quadrant II, and $\tan\frac{\pi}{3}=\sqrt{3}$, so $$\tan\frac{2\pi}{3}=-\sqrt{3}$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-\sqrt{3}$, $-\sqrt{3}$, has the correct magnitude and negative sign.
  • Choice B — $-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{3}$ is the reciprocal value.
  • Choice C — $\frac{\sqrt{3}}{3}$ is positive and also reciprocal.
  • Choice D — $\sqrt{3}$ is the quadrant I value, but the angle lands in quadrant II.
That matches Choice A — $-\sqrt{3}$.
